schema.description: Describes the request body of the /indexdocument API call

version.description: Version number for document for optimistic concurrency control. If absent or 0 then no version checks are done.

title.description: Document title, in plain text, if present. If not present, the title would be attempted to be extracted from the content.

filename.description: Source filename, in plain text, for the document. May be used as a fallback title for the document, if the title is not provided and cannot be extracted from the content. Populate this if there is no explicit title for the document and the content is sourced from a file.

container.description: The container name for the content (Folder for example for file content).

containerDatasourceId.description: This represents the datasource sepcific id of the container.

containerObjectType.description: This represents the object type of the container. It cannot have spaces or _

objectType.description: The type of the document (Case, KnowledgeArticle for Salesforce for example). It cannot have spaces or _

viewURL.description: The permalink for viewing the document. **Note: viewURL is a required field for non-entity datasources, but not required if the datasource is used to push custom entities (ie. datasources where isEntityDatasource is false).**'

id.description: The datasource specific id for the document. This field is case insensitive and should not be more than 200 characters in length. Note: id is a required field for datasources created after 1st March 2025

summary.description: Describes text content or base64 encoded binary content

textContent.description: text content. Only one of textContent or binary content can be specified

binaryContent.description: base64 encoded binary content. Only one of textContent or binary content can be specified

summary.title: ContentDefinition

body.description: Describes text content or base64 encoded binary content

textContent.description: text content. Only one of textContent or binary content can be specified

binaryContent.description: base64 encoded binary content. Only one of textContent or binary content can be specified

body.title: ContentDefinition

author.description: Describes how a user is referenced in a document. The user can be referenced by email or by a datasource specific id.

datasourceUserId.description: some datasources refer to the user by the datasource user id in the document

author.title: UserReferenceDefinition

owner.description: Describes how a user is referenced in a document. The user can be referenced by email or by a datasource specific id.

datasourceUserId.description: some datasources refer to the user by the datasource user id in the document

owner.title: UserReferenceDefinition

permissions.description: The permissions that define who can view this document in the search results. Please refer to [this](https://developers.glean.com/indexing/documents/permissions) for more details.

allowedUsers.description: List of users who can view the document

items.description: Describes how a user is referenced in a document. The user can be referenced by email or by a datasource specific id.

datasourceUserId.description: some datasources refer to the user by the datasource user id in the document

items.title: UserReferenceDefinition

allowedGroups.description: List of groups that can view the document

allowedGroupIntersections.description: List of allowed group intersections. This describes a permissions constraint of the form ((GroupA AND GroupB AND GroupC) OR (GroupX AND GroupY) OR ...

items.description: describes a list of groups that are all required in a permissions constraint
items.title: PermissionsGroupIntersectionDefinition

allowAnonymousAccess.description: If true, then any Glean user can view the document

allowAllDatasourceUsersAccess.description: If true, then any user who has an account in the datasource can view the document.

permissions.title: DocumentPermissionsDefinition

createdAt.description: The creation time, in epoch seconds.

updatedAt.description: The last update time, in epoch seconds.

updatedBy.description: Describes how a user is referenced in a document. The user can be referenced by email or by a datasource specific id.

datasourceUserId.description: some datasources refer to the user by the datasource user id in the document

updatedBy.title: UserReferenceDefinition

interactions.description: describes the interactions on the document
interactions.title: DocumentInteractionsDefinition

additionalUrls.description: Additional variations of the URL that this document points to.

nativeAppUrl.description: A deep link, if available, into the datasource's native application for the user's platform (e.g. slack://channel/message).

items.description: Describes a comment on a document

id.description: The document specific id for the comment. This field is case insensitive and should not be more than 200 characters in length.

author.description: Describes how a user is referenced in a document. The user can be referenced by email or by a datasource specific id.

datasourceUserId.description: some datasources refer to the user by the datasource user id in the document

author.title: UserReferenceDefinition

content.description: Describes text content or base64 encoded binary content

textContent.description: text content. Only one of textContent or binary content can be specified

binaryContent.description: base64 encoded binary content. Only one of textContent or binary content can be specified

content.title: ContentDefinition

createdAt.description: The creation time, in epoch seconds.

updatedAt.description: The last updated time, in epoch seconds.

updatedBy.description: Describes how a user is referenced in a document. The user can be referenced by email or by a datasource specific id.

datasourceUserId.description: some datasources refer to the user by the datasource user id in the document

updatedBy.title: UserReferenceDefinition

items.title: CommentDefinition

items.description: Describes the custom properties of the object.

value.description: Must either be a string or an array of strings. An integer, boolean, etc. is not valid. When OpenAPI Generator supports `oneOf`, we can semantically enforce this.

items.title: CustomProperty

customProperties.description: Additional metadata properties of the document. These can surface as [facets and operators](https://developers.glean.com/indexing/datasource/custom-properties/operators_and_facets).

schema.title: IndexDocumentRequest
